name: read_executed_notebook
description: Read a pre-executed Jupyter notebook (.ipynb, often named '*_executed.ipynb') and return its cells with printed outputs as structured JSON. Use this BEFORE reimplementing an analysis when the data folder ships with an executed notebook — the notebook already ran the authoritative pipeline with the exact package versions, filters, and thresholds that match the reference answers. Reimplementing with pydeseq2/scanpy/etc. produces different numbers. Accepts either a folder (auto-discovers the notebook) or a direct path. Optionally filters cells by a comma-separated search term list or a regex so the agent sees only the cells relevant to its question.

data_folder.description: Directory to search (recursively) for a .ipynb file. Prefers *_executed.ipynb over regular .ipynb. Provide either this OR notebook_path.

notebook_path.description: Direct path to a specific .ipynb file. Provide either this OR data_folder.

search.description: Optional: comma-separated terms (or regex if regex=true). Cells whose source or output contains any term are returned in 'matching_cells'. Case-insensitive. Example: 'upregulated,log2FoldChange' or 'padj.*0\.05'

regex.description: Treat 'search' as a Python regex instead of a comma-separated term list (default: false)

max_output_chars.description: Truncate each cell source/output to this many characters (default 400)

include_source.description: Include cell source code in the response (default true). Set false to halve response size when you only need outputs.
